notes: Spec asserts: session stays alive past readyTTL, state remains ready during idle, next user prompt resumes ready→working. Recording uses IRRLICHT_READY_SESSION_TTL=15s so the 15-second duration_at_least_ms below cleanly exceeds the TTL. If the daemon's PID liveness check regresses, the idle_window phase's invariants fire (no transcript_removed, no flicker to working). The 'second_turn_resume' phase proves the SAME session UUID survived — the validator matches on the matched session's id implicitly via the anchor chain.

text: Session appears in ready (pre-session or UUID, whichever arrives first)
First user prompt drives ready → working
First turn completes back to ready
Session does NOT disappear past readyTTL; state remains ready during idle
Next user prompt: ready → working resumes normally
Second turn completes back to ready
